title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens concluded his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Hagens is expected to turn pro soon, providing the Bruins a boost for the end of their regular season.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney kept trade options open, anticipating Hagens as a significant late-season addition.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' featuring Will Arnett, directed by Bradley Cooper, is a comedy-drama streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' set during WWII stars Cillian Murphy, available on Netflix.
'Jury Duty: Company Retreat' hidden camera comedy, starring improvisers and Anthony Norman, streams on Prime Video.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora enters his eighth season as Red Sox manager with his contract running through 2027.
Cora focused on the present, enjoying team atmosphere, and delaying discussions about his future post-2027.
Cora is poised to surpass Terry Francona with 134 more wins to become second in all-time wins for Red Sox managers.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line, featuring Arvidsson, Zacha, and Mittelstadt, has outscored opponents 35-16 this NHL season.
This line's goal differential is +19, second only to Colorado Avalanche's top line in the league.
Zacha, Arvidsson, and Mittelstadt are all experiencing career-best performances, contributing significantly to Boston's success.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH pediatric cancer care and research.
Mahr was inspired by young MGH patients during his last Boston Marathon in 2014, motivating his return in 2026.
Pediatric cancer receives limited funding compared to adult cancer, prompting Mahr to raise awareness and support.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposed Massachusetts income tax cut could reduce state revenue by $5.1 billion annually and save median households $1,250 per year.
78% of over 200 Boston.com poll respondents support the proposal to cut the state income tax rate from 5% to 4%.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing potential negative impacts on state finances and economic competitiveness.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze in Salisbury.
Prosecutors contend Church joked about poisoning Fowler and had a motive linked to his affair.
Defense argues Church's actions were misinterpreted and deny any intent to harm Fowler.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes lifting Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and expanding crossbow use to all hunters.
The proposal includes reducing bowhunting setbacks from 500 feet to 250 feet from dwellings, opening 40,000 acres of state land.
MassWildlife supports the changes to manage deer overpopulation, with 70% public comment favoring eliminating the Sunday hunting ban.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a pristine-looking derringer pistol in a case while magnet fishing in a Bridgewater river.
The YouTube video of the find has over 1,700 views, and Reddit posts about it gained 1.2 million views.
DeMontigny contacted police upon finding the pistol, which showed little wear despite the case's age.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old named Sergio Josue Castellanos faces a murder charge after a fatal stabbing in Chelsea.
Police responded to the incident near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue, finding multiple stabbing victims.
A 25-year-old Chelsea man died from his injuries during surgery; an investigation is ongoing.
